questions.text: The total aggregate of genes in a population at any one time is called the population's
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The total aggregate of genes in a population at any one time is called the population's "gene pool." This includes all the different alleles (alternative forms of genes) present in the population for each gene locus (specific location on a chromosome).

questions.text: Succession is a kind of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Succession is a kind of community relay in which assemblages of plants and animals replace the earlier ones in a sequence that is at least somewhat predictable.

questions.text: Establishment of new forests where no forests existed previously is called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option Forestation is the process of establishing or increasing forest cover through tree planting or natural regeneration.

The establishment of new forests where none existed previously is a process called "afforestation." Afforestation involves the planting of trees and other vegetation on land that has not historically been forested, such as degraded or barren land, agricultural fields, or urban areas.

It is not correct because Reforestation is the process of replanting trees in areas where forests have been depleted or removed, aiming to restore the forest cover that previously existed in those areas.

It is not correct because Deforestation refers to the clearing, destruction, or removal of forests or woodland, typically to make way for agricultural expansion, urbanization, infrastructure development, or commercial purposes.

questions.explanation: The establishment of new forests where none existed previously is a process called "afforestation." Afforestation involves the planting of trees and other vegetation on land that has not historically been forested, such as degraded or barren land, agricultural fields, or urban areas.

questions.text: Stomata are on the lower surface of leaves and located in depression in a type of plant
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option because they have no such adaptation.

Xerophytes have the adaptations for reduced rate of transpiration. Stomata are on lower surface of leaves and located in depression. Many xerophytes possess small, thick leaves to limit water loss by reducing surface area proportional to the volume. Their cuticle is thick, waxy and leathery.
It is not correct option because they have large stomata exposed to surface.
It is not correct option.

questions.explanation: Xerophytes have the adaptations for reduced rate of transpiration. Stomata are on lower surface of leaves and located in depression. Many xerophytes possess small, thick leaves to limit water loss by reducing surface area proportional to the volume. Their cuticle is thick, waxy and leathery.

questions.text: Minimum water is required for removal of wastes like
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct option because urea excretion generally requires more water.

Uric acid requires less water to be removed from body. This is because uric acid is less soluble in water and is excreted by animals as a semisolid paste or powder, particularly by species adapted to arid environments or those with limited access to water, such as reptiles (including birds) and insects.
It is not correct option because Creatinine is more soluble in water and is primarily excreted by the kidneys into the urine, which requires a relatively larger amount of water for its removal.

It is not correct option because Ammonia excretion typically requires the most water compared to other nitrogenous waste products. Ammonia is highly soluble in water but also highly toxic, so animals that excrete ammonia usually do so in aquatic environments where the ammonia can be quickly diluted and removed.

questions.explanation: Uric acid requires less water to be removed from body. This is because uric acid is less soluble in water and is excreted by animals as a semisolid paste or powder, particularly by species adapted to arid environments or those with limited access to water, such as reptiles (including birds) and insects.

questions.text: Mammals walk on the tips of toes modified into the hoof are called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct because in digitigrade locomotion animals walk on their toes.
It is not correct because plantigrade locomotion is characterized by the placement of the foot flat on the ground during each step, with the heel touching as well.

Mammals that walk on the tips of toes modified into hooves are called "unguligrade." This group includes animals such as horses, cattle, deer, giraffes, and many others. Hooves provide support and protection for these animals, allowing them to move efficiently across various terrains.
It s not correct because "Brachigrade" is a term used to describe mammals that have relatively short limbs compared to their body size.

questions.explanation: Mammals that walk on the tips of toes modified into hooves are called "unguligrade." This group includes animals such as horses, cattle, deer, giraffes, and many others. Hooves provide support and protection for these animals, allowing them to move efficiently across various terrains.

questions.text: Slightly elastic connective tissues that attach bone to bone are called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The brachialis is a muscle located in the upper arm. so it is not correct option.
The brachioradialis is a muscle located in the forearm. so it is not correct option.
Tendons attach muscles to bones so it is not correct option.

Slightly elastic connective tissues that attach bone to bone are called "ligaments." Ligaments are tough, fibrous bands composed mainly of collagen fibers, which provide strength and stability to joints by connecting bones and preventing excessive movement or dislocation.

questions.explanation: Slightly elastic connective tissues that attach bone to bone are called "ligaments." Ligaments are tough, fibrous bands composed mainly of collagen fibers, which provide strength and stability to joints by connecting bones and preventing excessive movement or dislocation.

questions.text: Fruit ripening is often accompanied by a burst of respiratory activity called
questions.subject: Biology

"Dimetric" can refer to the arrangement of leaves or branches in plants. so it is not correct.

questions.text: Phragmoplast is formed by vesicles which originate from
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ribosomes are involved in protein synthesis.
It is not correct because lysosomes are related to killing and engulfing activities.

The phragmoplast is formed during plant cell division and consists of vesicles derived from the Golgi apparatus. These vesicles contain cell wall materials such as cellulose and other polysaccharides.
Mesosomes are folded invagination of the plasma membrane in prokaryotic cells, involved in processes such as DNA replication and cell division so it is not correct option.

questions.explanation: The phragmoplast is formed during plant cell division and consists of vesicles derived from the Golgi apparatus. These vesicles contain cell wall materials such as cellulose and other polysaccharides.

questions.text: During meiosis crossing over occurs in stage
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is not correct because in this phase the chromosomes become visible, shorten and thick.
It is not correct because in this phase pairing of homologous chromosomes called synapsis starts.
It is not correct because during this phase the condensation of chromosomes reaches to its maximum.

During meiosis, crossing over occurs during Prophase I, specifically in the substage called "pachytene." This is when homologous chromosomes pair up and exchange genetic material, resulting in genetic recombination.

questions.explanation: During meiosis, crossing over occurs during Prophase I, specifically in the substage called "pachytene." This is when homologous chromosomes pair up and exchange genetic material, resulting in genetic recombination.

questions.text: The association of indifferent or situations without patent reward is called
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Latent learning is defined as the association of indifferent stimuli or situations without patent reward.
Insight learning refers to a type of problem-solving or learning characterized by sudden realization, understanding, or comprehension of a solution to a problem. so not a correct option.
Imprinting is a form of learning that occurs during a critical period early in an animal's life and involves the rapid and relatively permanent acquisition of certain behaviors. So not correct.
Habituation is a form of learning in which an organism becomes accustomed to a repeated stimulus and gradually decreases its response to it over time. So not correct option.

questions.explanation: Latent learning is defined as the association of indifferent stimuli or situations without patent reward.

questions.text: Unspecialized cells present in flatworms and Planaria are
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Planaria and flatworms possess unspecialized cells called "neoblasts." These cells are multipotent stem cells capable of differentiating into various cell types, enabling regeneration and repair of tissues and organs in these organisms.
It is not correct because osteoblasts are specialized cells responsible for the formation of new bone tissue through a process called osteogenesis or ossification.
It is not correct because osteoclasts are specialized cells responsible for the breakdown and resorption of bone tissue.
It is not correct because chondrocyte is a specialized cell found in cartilage tissue.

questions.explanation: Planaria and flatworms possess unspecialized cells called "neoblasts." These cells are multipotent stem cells capable of differentiating into various cell types, enabling regeneration and repair of tissues and organs in these organisms.

questions.subject: Biology
